data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-5ae050dc">
    <title>graphql LAYER 3: implement Bead/Execution Node(id) Relay resolution with project-aware lookups</title>
    <description>
LAYER 3 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. Completeness work — fixes incomplete Relay spec compliance.

PROBLEM
cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Node(id) at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go resolves Bead IDs (prefix "bead-") via the bead store scoped to context WorkingDir.
2. Node(id) resolves Execution run IDs (prefix "exec-" or whatever the run-id format is) via the executions store scoped to context WorkingDir.
3. Returns nil + appropriate error for IDs that don't match any known prefix.
4. Test: TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject — register two projects with overlapping bead IDs; query Node("bead-X") via project A's scoped GraphQL; assert returns project A's bead, not B's.
5. Test: TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject — same shape for execution runs.
6. Test: TestNode_ReturnsNilForUnknownPrefix — assert no panic, nil returned.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:server, area:graphql, kind:feature</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="aa2cd8c12330721168d5f95baa706eb459f4bee3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: You're out of extra usage · resets 11:40pm (America/New_York)
